title: Faculty Affairs | Duke Kunshan University
text:
Office of Faculty Affairs
Overview
The Office of Faculty Affairs, working closely with various functional offices at Duke Kunshan University, supports all faculty in their scholarship, teaching and research, internal and external engagement, professional development, and personal challenges, so that they can focus on achieving excellence.
Roles and Responsibilities
Support Faculty Developmental Initiatives
The Office of Faculty Affairs supports faculty development initiatives regarding reappointment, tenure, promotion, Duke-DKU Faculty Mentoring Program, annual faculty evaluation, pre-tenure sabbatical application, etc.
Reappointment Orientation
Reappointment Orientation
The orientation aims to provide instructions for preparation of dossiers for tenure/non-tenure track faculty reappointment and provide guidelines for formatting dossiers for reappointment.
Tenure Experience Sharing Workshop
The workshop invites faculty across DKU’s academic units who have been tenured to share their personal experiences and valuable insights regarding the tenure process.
Pre-Tenure Sabbatical Sharing Workshop
The application procedures, individual experiences and best practices for a successful sabbatical are shared during the workshop.
Duke-DKU Faculty Mentoring Program
The program is initiated to provide guidance by a group of senior Duke faculty to junior DKU faculty to support their personal professional development towards tenure.
New Faculty Orientation
The New Faculty Orientation sessions are held to welcome new faculty members and give them an introduction to DKU curriculum, guidelines, resources and functions of different offices.
Annual Evaluation Retreat
This event serves as a platform for all faculty annual report reviewers to engage in discussions about the evaluation process, criteria, feedback and share their best practices.
Women in Academic Retreat
This event is organized to raise awareness of issues concerning women in all areas of higher education, foster a thriving environment for women career development and leadership recognition, and promote holistic well-being of women at DKU.
Administer Internal Faculty Grants
The Office of Faculty Affairs administers DKU’s internal funding opportunities for faculty research and professional development.
Summer Research Program Grant (Annually)
Summer Research Program Grant (Annually)
The grant supports new initiatives for significant contributions to research, creative practice and higher education.
DKU-Duke Collaborative Grant (Annually)
The grant encourages the engagement of DKU faculty (particularly junior faculty) with colleagues at Duke to foster collaborative academic or creative endeavors.
DKU-Duke Faculty Travel Grant (Annually)
The grant provides partial support of travel expenses for DKU faculty to work on a project and increase networking opportunities at Duke.
Faculty Scholarship and Travel Grant (Annually)
The grant helps faculty members to develop new projects of research or to complete a current project, which will lead to publications or other products (e.g., artistic installations, performances, or productions).
Collaborate on Faculty Development
The Office of Faculty Affairs works with faculty assembly committees and non-faculty assembly committees on faculty development.
Coordinate Policy Development and Handbook Updates
The Office coordinates faculty-related policy development and ensures the DKU Faculty Handbook remains up to date.
Contact Us
Office of Faculty Affairs
dku_facultyaffairs@dukekunshan.edu.cn
Director of Faculty Development
marcus.werner@dukekunshan.edu.cn
Senior Faculty Support Officer
zhongqiu.shen@dukekunshan.edu.cn
Resources
Faculty Development
Tenure and Promotion

text_blocks.text: The Office of Faculty Affairs, working closely with various functional offices at Duke Kunshan University, supports all faculty in their scholarship, teaching and research, internal and external engagement, professional development, and personal challenges, so that they can focus on achieving excellence.

The Office of Faculty Affairs supports faculty development initiatives regarding reappointment, tenure, promotion, Duke-DKU Faculty Mentoring Program, annual faculty evaluation, pre-tenure sabbatical application, etc.
The orientation aims to provide instructions for preparation of dossiers for tenure/non-tenure track faculty reappointment and provide guidelines for formatting dossiers for reappointment.
The workshop invites faculty across DKU’s academic units who have been tenured to share their personal experiences and valuable insights regarding the tenure process.
The application procedures, individual experiences and best practices for a successful sabbatical are shared during the workshop.
The program is initiated to provide guidance by a group of senior Duke faculty to junior DKU faculty to support their personal professional development towards tenure.
The New Faculty Orientation sessions are held to welcome new faculty members and give them an introduction to DKU curriculum, guidelines, resources and functions of different offices.
This event serves as a platform for all faculty annual report reviewers to engage in discussions about the evaluation process, criteria, feedback and share their best practices.

This event is organized to raise awareness of issues concerning women in all areas of higher education, foster a thriving environment for women career development and leadership recognition, and promote holistic well-being of women at DKU.
The Office of Faculty Affairs administers DKU’s internal funding opportunities for faculty research and professional development.
The grant supports new initiatives for significant contributions to research, creative practice and higher education.
The grant encourages the engagement of DKU faculty (particularly junior faculty) with colleagues at Duke to foster collaborative academic or creative endeavors.
The grant provides partial support of travel expenses for DKU faculty to work on a project and increase networking opportunities at Duke.

The grant helps faculty members to develop new projects of research or to complete a current project, which will lead to publications or other products (e.g., artistic installations, performances, or productions).
The Office of Faculty Affairs works with faculty assembly committees and non-faculty assembly committees on faculty development.
Coordinate Policy Development and Handbook Updates
The Office coordinates faculty-related policy development and ensures the DKU Faculty Handbook remains up to date.
